routes_and_endpoints.flag: reachable by anonymous while the page embedding it (arena/) 404s them
no chef_battle_guard/is_battle_visible check at all, unlike the page (battle_detail) it is attached to

routes_and_endpoints.superuser: 200 only if owner-slug OR (ARENA_MASTER_CONSOLE_ENABLED and author.has_arena_console_access), else 404
same rule as master/; owner-only writes re-checked inside per DG-02 (lines 2773-2777)

guard_functions.is_battle_visible: chef_battle/access.py:11-44 -- True if settings.CHEF_BATTLE_ENABLED, OR user.is_staff/is_superuser, OR user simply has a recipe_author_profile at all (lines 43-44) -- ANY registered author, staff or not.
guard_functions.has_arena_console_access: chef_battle/access.py:47-66 -- requires is_superuser AND a RecipeAuthor AND (author.slug == settings.OWNER_SLUG OR (settings.ARENA_MASTER_CONSOLE_ENABLED AND author.has_arena_console_access))
guard_functions.settings: config/settings.py:322 OWNER_SLUG='greenbear'; :334 CHEF_BATTLE_ENABLED default False; :338 ARENA_MASTER_CONSOLE_ENABLED default False

contract_deviation.documented_requirement: docs/CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md:123-138 -- arena_visibility.recipe_author_without_staff must be false pre-release; 'If production behaviour differs, treat it as a release-gate defect.'
contract_deviation.actual_code: chef_battle/access.py:16-20,43-44 implements recipe_author_without_staff = true, per an inline comment citing an owner decision dated 2026-07-20
contract_deviation.test_confirming_it: chef_battle/tests.py:5190 ArenaDarkLaunchTests.test_plain_author_sees_the_arena_during_dark_launch -- passing, confirms 200 for a plain non-staff author
contract_deviation.scope: Applies to Arena Hall + Battle Room (everything gated purely by chef_battle_guard/is_battle_visible). Does NOT apply to Arena Master Console, which remains superuser+owner/flag-only via the separate has_arena_console_access gate.
contract_deviation.classification: This is a release-gate defect per the contract's own escalation rule, OR a superseding owner decision not yet reflected in the contract document -- Bolt did not modify code or docs; flagging per audit scope, not resolving.

additional_gaps: arena_react (views.py:3159) has no visibility/auth gate at all -- docstring says 'Public but rate-limited' (line 3161). Reachable by fully anonymous users while the embedding page (arena/) 404s them for the same anonymous visitor.

battle_chat_send and battle_chat_poll (views.py:2020, 2056) have no chef_battle_guard/is_battle_visible check, unlike battle_detail which gates the page they're attached to. Anonymous or non-eligible visitors can POST/poll chat for any battle ID even while battle_detail for that battle 404s them.

initial_context_defect.actual_render_context_keys: rank_groups, spectator_count, active_battle, arena_data, viewer_author, user_enrolled (render() call, lines 1135-1142)
initial_context_defect.nesting: crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts ARE present in the payload (lines 1054-1056) but only nested inside arena_data (lines 1095-1108) -- never at top level of the render context
initial_context_defect.effect: Django silently resolves the undefined top-level variables to empty. {{ crown_streak|default:0 }} always renders '0'; the crown_ladder and recent_gifts loops always iterate zero items and fall through to their {% empty %} branches ('No crowns have been awarded today.' / 'No battle gifts have been delivered yet.') on every initial page load, regardless of real data, until the first arena_state poll overwrites the DOM via JS. No error, no 500, nothing logged -- silent and permanent on first paint.
initial_context_defect.scope_note: arena_master_console.html does not reference crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts anywhere -- this defect is isolated to the public Arena Hall template.
initial_context_defect.existing_test_gap: chef_battle/tests.py:6607-6628 ArenaPayloadWiringTests.test_payload_has_arena_panels only asserts the raw _build_arena_payload() dict has these keys -- never renders arena.html or inspects actual template context/HTML output.
initial_context_defect.minimal_safe_fix_contract_not_implemente: Either (a) add crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts to the arena() view's top-level render context alongside arena_data, matching what the template already expects, or (b) change the template to read arena_data.crown_streak / arena_data.crown_ladder / arena_data.recent_gifts to match the existing nested contract. No code changed by this audit.

privileged_preview.note: No ?proto=1-gated code path exists in current views.py (grep for 'proto' returns no matches). _arena_render_ring.html now renders unconditionally for every visitor who passes chef_battle_guard (arena.html:120), regardless of any proto query param.
privileged_preview.stale_test: chef_battle/tests.py:2395-2409 test_procedural_arena_is_explicit_proto_gate still requests ?proto=1 and asserts 200/content, but only because that content now renders unconditionally -- test name is misleading, there is no proto gate anymore. Currently passing (not a failure), just mislabeled.

demo_vs_preview.leak_to_non_staff: Not observed -- gated on is_moderator(request.user) in addition to the outer chef_battle_guard; if condition fails for non-moderators, arena_data['center'] is left unmodified and the normal arena renders.

master_live_arena_previews.csrf: No csrf_exempt applied to any of these (csrf_exempt is only used at token_stripe_webhook, views.py:686) -- Django's default CSRF protection applies to their POST endpoints.
master_live_arena_previews.method_restrictions: live_arena_preview has no explicit @require_GET but performs no writes, only render(); the two write endpoints correctly use @require_POST.

polling_contracts.arena_state_endpoint: chef_battle/views.py:1163-1201, returns top-level bare keys: rings, spectators, center, latest_result, crown_streak, crown_ladder, recent_gifts, metrics, phase, deadline, geometry, server_time -- sourced from the same _build_arena_payload()
polling_contracts.initial_context_shape: arena() view nests the identical key set one level under arena_data (lines 1095-1108), then json_script's the whole arena_data dict into the DOM at templates/chef_battle/_arena_render_ring.html:20
polling_contracts.mismatch: Polling response is a flat dict (crown_streak bare); initial-render JS blob nests the same fields one level under arena_data (arena_data.crown_streak). The arena.html template's structural reads (arena_data.phase, arena_data.metrics, arena_data.center, arena_data.deadline) correctly use the nested initial-context shape, but its crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts reads use the bare/top-level name that only exists in the arena_state POLLING JSON contract, not in the initial arena_data contract -- i.e. those three template lines look like they were written against the wrong contract.
polling_contracts.existing_test_gap: chef_battle/tests.py:2623 ArenaMasterStateTests.test_public_arena_state_keys_unchanged verifies arena_state's own JSON key set hasn't drifted, but does not cross-check against the initial arena_data template context, so it would not catch this mismatch.

_arena_render_ring.html.contract_note: docs/CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md:120-121 and chef_battle/access.py:33-34 both state this shared renderer must not be removed until the Master Console is decoupled or has a compatible replacement -- not touched by this audit.

targeted_tests.command: venv/Scripts/python.exe manage.py test chef_battle.tests.ChefBattleAccessTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaDarkLaunchTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaMasterConsoleAccessTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaMasterStateTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaMasterActionTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaDataSelectorsTests chef_battle.tests.ArenaPayloadWiringTests -v 2
targeted_tests.environment: local SQLite dev settings (in-memory test DB), not production
targeted_tests.note: This is a targeted subset per the audit scope, not the full ~1500-test suite. No duplicate full-suite run performed. Not coordinated as a shard with GreenBear/Ember -- ad hoc local run for this audit only.

findings.title: Arena Hall/Battle Room access contract deviates from docs/CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md
findings.detail: Contract requires recipe_author_without_staff: false pre-release; actual code (chef_battle/access.py:43-44) grants access to any registered author regardless of staff status, per an inline comment citing an owner decision dated 2026-07-20. Confirmed by a passing test (ArenaDarkLaunchTests.test_plain_author_sees_the_arena_during_dark_launch). The contract document itself says this exact situation should be 'treat[ed] as a release-gate defect' if production differs -- but there is also comment evidence this was an explicit later owner decision. Owner Product Owner needs to confirm which is current: the contract, or the code comment, and reconcile the written contract if the code is intentional.

findings.title: arena_react has no visibility/auth gate
findings.detail: views.py:3159, docstring says 'Public but rate-limited'. Anonymous users can hit this endpoint while the Arena Hall page itself 404s them during dark launch. Scope/intent unclear -- flagging, not fixing.

findings.title: battle_chat_send / battle_chat_poll have no chef_battle_guard check
findings.detail: views.py:2020, 2056. Unlike battle_detail (the page these are attached to), these endpoints have no visibility gate -- anonymous/non-eligible users can post ('Anonymous') and poll chat for any battle ID even when battle_detail 404s them for that same battle.

findings.title: Initial-render context defect: crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts always empty on first paint
findings.detail: arena.html reads these as top-level variables; the arena() view only ever nests them under arena_data. Silent, permanent wrong-data bug on every initial page load until the first JS poll. See initial_context_defect section for full detail and minimal fix contract (not implemented).

findings.title: test_procedural_arena_is_explicit_proto_gate is a stale/misleading test name
findings.detail: chef_battle/tests.py:2395-2409 -- no proto gate exists anymore in the code; the test still passes only because the content it checks now renders unconditionally. Not a functional bug, but the test name and any documentation referencing a ?proto=1 gate are stale.

findings.title: arena_state vs initial arena_data payload shape mismatch
findings.detail: Polling JSON is flat; initial-render JS blob is nested under arena_data. Plausible root cause tying into F4 -- the three affected template lines appear to have been written against the polling contract instead of the initial-render contract.

recommended_implementation_packages.scope: Either add crown_streak/crown_ladder/recent_gifts to arena()'s top-level render context, or change arena.html to read them via arena_data.*. Add a template-render-level test asserting real (non-empty, non-default) values appear in rendered HTML on first paint, not just in the raw payload dict.
recommended_implementation_packages.owner_decision_needed: Which side of the contract (view vs template) is authoritative going forward.

recommended_implementation_packages.package: Reconcile F1 -- product contract vs implemented author-visibility rule
recommended_implementation_packages.scope: Either update docs/CHEF_BATTLE_PRODUCT_CONTRACT_2D.md's arena_visibility matrix to reflect the 2026-07-20 owner decision (author-without-staff: true), or correct the code back to the written contract. Pure documentation/decision-recording, or a code change -- Product Owner call.
recommended_implementation_packages.owner_decision_needed: Confirm which is current and update the losing side.

recommended_implementation_packages.package: Close the F2/F3 gate gaps on arena_react and battle_chat_send/poll
recommended_implementation_packages.scope: Decide whether these should be gated by chef_battle_guard/is_battle_visible like their sibling pages, or are intentionally public; add explicit tests either way.
recommended_implementation_packages.owner_decision_needed: Intent confirmation before any code change.

recommended_implementation_packages.scope: Low priority; rename test_procedural_arena_is_explicit_proto_gate to reflect that content renders unconditionally now, and check for stale ?proto=1 references elsewhere in docs.
